name: Country Music
description: Nashville legends, Grand Ole Opry, and country classics

questions.q: Which country legend wrote and originally recorded 'I Will Always Love You'?

source.notes: Written 1973 about her split from mentor Porter Wagoner. Whitney Houston's 1992 cover was the blockbuster.

questions.q: What was the nickname given to Johnny Cash?

source.notes: For his preference for black stage attire. Born in Kingsland, Arkansas, 1932.

questions.q: Which country song made famous by Johnny Cash begins 'Love is a burnin' thing'?

source.notes: Written by June Carter and Merle Kilgore. Released 1963. Cash's signature song.

questions.q: What is the name of Willie Nelson's famous Martin guitar?

source.notes: A Martin N-20 classical guitar. Named after Roy Rogers's horse. Played since 1969.

questions.q: Which Oklahoma-born country superstar's 1990 album 'No Fences' made him one of the best-selling artists of all time?

source.notes: Sold 17+ million US copies. Features 'Friends in Low Places.'

questions.q: Which country legend recorded 'Crazy' (written by Willie Nelson) in 1961, her signature hit?

source.notes: Died in a plane crash in 1963 at age 30. Inducted posthumously into the Country Music Hall of Fame.

questions.q: Which country trio (formerly known as the Dixie Chicks) had the 1998 hit 'Wide Open Spaces'?

source.notes: Rebranded as 'The Chicks' in 2020. Natalie Maines, Martie Maguire, Emily Strayer.

questions.q: Which actor won an Oscar for playing Johnny Cash in the 2005 biopic 'Walk the Line'?

source.notes: Reese Witherspoon won for playing June Carter. Phoenix was nominated but didn't win for his role.

questions.q: Which country singer starred alongside Blake Shelton on 'The Voice' and married him in 2021?

source.notes: Stefani is actually a pop artist, not country. Miranda Lambert was Shelton's previous wife (2011-2015).

questions.q: Which country queen won the 2005 'American Idol' and went on to a major Grammy-winning career?

source.notes: Season 4 winner. Later married NHL player Mike Fisher.

questions.q: Which Canadian country-pop singer's 1997 album 'Come On Over' became the best-selling album by a female artist in history?

source.notes: 40+ million copies sold. Features 'You're Still the One' and 'Man! I Feel Like a Woman!'

questions.q: Dolly Parton's theme park in Tennessee is named what?

source.notes: Located in Pigeon Forge, TN. Opened 1986. 3M+ visitors annually.

questions.q: Who is known as 'The Man in Black' in country music?

source.notes: The 1971 song 'Man in Black' explains his wardrobe as a protest uniform.

questions.q: What does the acronym CMA stand for in country music?

source.notes: Founded 1958. Hosts the annual CMA Awards. Based in Nashville.

questions.q: Which 1970s trucker song anthem by C.W. McCall became a huge crossover country/pop hit?

source.notes: Released 1975. Inspired the 1978 film 'Convoy' directed by Sam Peckinpah.

questions.q: Which country music legend is famous for the song 'Coal Miner's Daughter' and autobiography of the same name?

source.notes: Born in Butcher Hollow, Kentucky. Sissy Spacek won an Oscar for portraying her in the 1980 film.

questions.q: Which early country star, considered the 'Father of Country Music,' died in 1933?

source.notes: Known as 'The Singing Brakeman.' Died of tuberculosis. Hank Williams died 1953.

questions.q: Who sang the 1975 crossover hit 'Rhinestone Cowboy'?

source.notes: Campbell's biggest hit. Also featured in 'Dallas Buyers Club.'

questions.q: What historic Nashville venue is known as the 'Mother Church of Country Music'?

source.notes: Built 1892. Home of the Grand Ole Opry from 1943-1974. Still used for performances.

questions.q: Which country superstar was born Troyal Garth Brooks in 1962?

source.notes: Born February 7, 1962 in Tulsa, Oklahoma.
